      • websocketUpgrade(res: Response; req: HttpRequest; server: HttpServer = nil; onOpen: WsOpenCb = nil; onMessage: WsMessageCb = nil; onClose: WsCloseCb = nil; onError: WsErrorCb = nil): WsConnection
        writeFrame
      • writeFrame(conn: Connection; opcode: int; payload: openArray[byte])
        writeFrameMasked
      • writeFrameMasked(conn: Connection; opcode: int; payload: openArray[byte]; mask: array[4, uint8])

RFC 6455 WebSocket server.

Two modes of operation:

1. Standalone — dedicated WebSocket server on its own TCP port:

   let loop = newLoop()
   let wss = newWsServer(loop)
   wss.onOpen do (ws: WsConnection):
     echo "client connected"
   wss.onMessage do (ws: WsConnection, kind: WsFrameKind, data: openArray[byte]):
     ws.sendText("echo: " & cast[string](@data))
   wss.listen("0.0.0.0", 9001)
   loop.run()

2. Upgraded from HttpServer — route handler performs the handshake:

   server.get("/ws") do (req: HttpRequest, res: Response):
     websocketUpgrade(res, req, onOpen, onMessage, onClose)

Imports

../net/tcp, ../loop, ../types, http, httpserver

Types

WsCloseCb = proc (ws: WsConnection; code: int; reason: string) {.closure.}
WsConnection = ref object
  conn*: Connection          ## Underlying TCP connection
A WebSocket connection. Wraps a TCP connection with frame parsing, fragmentation reassembly, and control frame handling. 
WsErrorCb = proc (ws: WsConnection; err: string) {.closure.}
WsFrameKind = enum
  wsContinuation = 0, wsText = 1, wsBinary = 2, wsClose = 8, wsPing = 9,
  wsPong = 10
WebSocket frame opcodes. 
WsMessageCb = proc (ws: WsConnection; kind: WsFrameKind; data: openArray[byte]) {.
    closure.}
WsOpenCb = proc (ws: WsConnection) {.closure.}
WsServer = ref object
Standalone WebSocket server.

Procs

proc buildHandshakeResponse(acceptKey: string): string {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Build the HTTP 101 Switching Protocols response for a WebSocket upgrade. 
proc close(wss: WsServer) {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Shut down the WebSocket server. 
proc closeWs(ws: WsConnection; code: int = 1000; reason: string = "") {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Send a close frame and shut down the connection. 
proc computeAcceptKey(clientKey: string): string {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Compute the Sec-WebSocket-Accept value from the client's key. 
proc listen(wss: WsServer; address: string; port: int) {.
    ...raises: [NetError, KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Bind and start accepting WebSocket connections on a dedicated port. 
proc newWsServer(loop: Loop): WsServer {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a standalone WebSocket server. Register callbacks then call listen(). 
proc onClose(wss: WsServer; cb: WsCloseCb) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Set the callback for closed connections. 
proc onError(wss: WsServer; cb: WsErrorCb) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Set the callback for errors. 
proc onMessage(wss: WsServer; cb: WsMessageCb) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Set the callback for incoming messages. 
proc onOpen(wss: WsServer; cb: WsOpenCb) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Set the callback for new WebSocket connections. 
proc reset(p: WsFrameParser) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Reset the parser for the next frame. 
proc sendBinary(ws: WsConnection; data: openArray[byte]) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Send a binary message. 
proc sendHandshake(conn: Connection; clientKey: string) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Send the HTTP 101 upgrade response directly on the connection. 
proc sendPing(ws: WsConnection; data: openArray[byte] = []) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Send a ping frame. 
proc sendPong(ws: WsConnection; data: openArray[byte] = []) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Send a pong frame (usually automatic, but exposed for manual use). 
proc sendText(ws: WsConnection; s: string) {....raises: [KeyError, OSError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Send a text message. 
proc websocketUpgrade(res: Response; req: HttpRequest; server: HttpServer = nil;
                      onOpen: WsOpenCb = nil; onMessage: WsMessageCb = nil;
                      onClose: WsCloseCb = nil; onError: WsErrorCb = nil): WsConnection {.
    discardable, ...raises: [KeyError, OSError, Exception],
    tags: [WriteDirEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}

Upgrade an HTTP connection to WebSocket. Call this from an HTTP route handler.

After the upgrade, the connection is no longer managed by the HttpServer — all future data goes directly to the WebSocket callbacks.

Pass the server argument to clean up the HTTP session tracking.

server.get("/ws") do (req: HttpRequest, res: Response):
  let ws = websocketUpgrade(res, req, server,
    onOpen = proc(ws: WsConnection) = echo "open!",
    onMessage = proc(ws: WsConnection, kind: WsFrameKind, data: openArray[byte]) =
      ws.sendText(cast[string](@data)),
  )
proc writeFrame(conn: Connection; opcode: int; payload: openArray[byte]) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Write a single WebSocket frame to the connection. Server frames are never masked (RFC 6455 §5.1). 
proc writeFrameMasked(conn: Connection; opcode: int; payload: openArray[byte];
                      mask: array[4, uint8]) {....raises: [KeyError, OSError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Write a masked WebSocket frame (for client-to-server; servers don't mask).
